title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (May 27)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: Abington's 28 Linda St., a one-family ranch built in 1951, sold for $455,000.
Acton featured a Colonial on Perkins Lane selling for $1,266,000; built in 1995, it has 4 bedrooms.
Boston's 288 Commonwealth Ave. #3 condo, built in 1899, sold for $9,750,000; it includes 3 bedrooms and 5 baths.

title: 4 Patriots roster positional battles to follow during OTAs
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: New England Patriots' OTAs highlight offensive tackle and running back depth for potential roster changes.
First-round pick Caleb Lomu may compete for offensive tackle roles amid Moses' age and Campbell's past struggles.
Running back Jam Miller and others compete for third spot after Patriots' departure from Antonio Gibson.

title: Former Stoughton cop accused of killing Sandra Birchmore denied bail pending trial
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Matthew Farwell, former Stoughton cop, denied bail in connection to Sandra Birchmore's 2021 alleged murder.
Judge M. Page Kelley cited 'strong' evidence and potential flight risk in decision to keep Farwell detained.
Prosecutors allege Farwell staged Birchmore's death as suicide; DNA testing showed he wasn't her unborn child's father.

title: Joe Mazzulla named NBA Coach of the Year for 2025-26 season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Joe Mazzulla named NBA Coach of the Year, first Celtics coach honored since Bill Fitch in 1980.
Mazzulla led Boston Celtics to 56 wins despite initial low expectations for the 2025-26 season.
The Celtics were eliminated in the first playoff round after losing a 3-1 series lead to the 76ers.

title: New Hampshire mother and son found dead in suspected murder-suicide
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Eileen Shevelin, 78, and her son Thomas Wallace, 57, were found dead in their Dover, N.H. home.
Wallace suffered a gunshot wound to the head; authorities suspect a murder-suicide.
Autopsies will be conducted in Concord, N.H., and there is no public threat, officials confirmed.

title: Red Sox get good news on Roman Anthony, Garrett Crochet’s injuries
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Roman Anthony progressed from a right-hand injury, feeling better after taking 12-15 dry swings.
Garrett Crochet, recovering from left shoulder inflammation, pitched one inning of live batting practice with mid-90s velocity.
No return dates set for Anthony or Crochet, though both show positive recovery signs, says interim manager Chad Tracy.

title: Mass. police respond after ‘body’ reported in truck, turns out to be a mannequin
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Wakefield police investigated a 911 call reporting a dead body in a truck, which turned out to be a mannequin.
The mannequin in Wakefield was a dummy used for fire and rescue training, not a deceased person.
Police emphasized on Facebook that despite reports, no dead body was found in a Wakefield truck on May 19.

title: Man spent hours between wall and elevator shaft at Fields Corner MBTA station, police say
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A man was found stuck between a wall and elevator shaft at Fields Corner MBTA station in Dorchester.
The man, claiming to be homeless, broke into the area seeking shelter and was rescued after several hours.
Witnesses reported the situation to authorities after hearing loud noises; the man faces a trespassing charge.

title: 2-year-old bitten by raccoon at Mass. town beach
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 2-year-old was bitten by a raccoon at Wayland Town Beach while playing on a jungle gym.
Grayson Cohen received three stitches and began a rabies vaccination series at Newton-Wellesley Hospital.
Wayland Police have not located the raccoon and advise caution around wild animals in the area.

title: 3 Boston cheese shops perfect for the ultimate summer picnic spread
author: Kelly Egan
bullets: American Provisions in South Boston offers artisan cheeses and charcuterie, with picnic recommendations like Drunken Goat cheese.
Bacco’s Wine and Cheese in Back Bay features global cheese selections and picnic boxes, with Lillian cheese as a staff favorite.
Crane River Cheese Club in Beacon Hill focuses on New England products, offering seasonally rotating Cheese Club Kits.
